description_en: A person who makes a formal application for something, especially a job.
example: There were over 200 applicants for the junior developer position.

description_en: An act of assessing something or someone.
example: His annual performance appraisal was very positive.

description_en: An amount of money added to a person's wages as a reward for good performance.
example: Employees receive a performance-based bonus at the end of the year.

description_en: State of emotional, physical, and mental exhaustion caused by excessive and prolonged stress.
example: Working 80 hours a week is a direct path to burnout.

description_en: Something, typically money, awarded to someone as a recompense for loss, injury, or suffering.
example: The total compensation package includes salary, health insurance, and stock options.

description_en: The latest time or date by which something should be completed.
example: We are working hard to meet the Friday deadline.

description_en: The assignment of any authority to another person to carry out specific activities.
example: Effective delegation is a key skill for any manager.

description_en: The state of having paid work.
example: Overall employment rates have increased this quarter.

description_en: The quality of bending easily or the ability to be easily modified.
example: Remote work offers more flexibility in balancing work and life.

description_en: A real or imagined wrong or other cause for complaint or protest, especially unfair treatment.
example: The employee filed a grievance regarding his unfair dismissal.

description_en: The place or building serving as the administrative center of an organization.
example: The company's headquarters are located in New York City.

description_en: A formal meeting in which one or more persons question, consult, or evaluate another person.
example: I have a job interview for a marketing role tomorrow morning.

description_en: A worker or sports player who is reliable but not brilliant.
example: He's a reliable journeyman plumber who knows his trade well.

description_en: Someone whose job involves handling or using information.
example: Software engineers and accountants are examples of knowledge workers.

meaning: sa thải (do tinh giảm biên chế)
description_en: A discharge of a worker or workers because of economic conditions or a shortage of work.
example: The tech company announced a 10% layoff due to falling revenues.
note: Not due to poor individual performance.

description_en: An experienced and trusted adviser.
example: She has been a mentor to many young engineers in the company.

description_en: The action or process of interacting with others to exchange information and develop professional or social contacts.
example: Networking is essential for advancing in your career.

description_en: The action or process of integrating a new employee into an organization.
example: The company has a very structured onboarding process for new hires.

description_en: A regular payment made during a person's retirement from an investment fund to which that person or their employer has contributed.
example: He will receive a comfortable pension when he retires next year.

description_en: A quality or accomplishment that makes someone suitable for a particular job or activity.
example: A PhD is a minimum qualification for this research position.

description_en: The action of finding new people to join an organization or body.
example: The recruitment process can take up to three months.

description_en: A fixed regular payment, typically paid on a monthly basis but often expressed as an annual sum, made by an employer to an employee.
example: My annual salary is $60,000.

description_en: The action of ending a connection or relationship.
example: He received a generous severance package after his contract was terminated.
note: Payment made upon leaving a company.

meaning: tỷ lệ nghỉ việc; doanh số
description_en: The rate at which employees leave a workforce and are replaced.
example: High employee turnover can be a sign of a toxic workplace culture.

description_en: The process of learning new skills or of teaching workers new skills.
example: Upskilling is necessary to stay relevant in the age of AI.

description_en: An unoccupied position or job.
example: There is a vacancy for an assistant manager in the sales department.

description_en: The amount of work to be done by someone or something.
example: The engineering team has a heavy workload this quarter.

description_en: The difference between the actual behavior of a firm and that predicted by economic theory.
example: Improving x-efficiency is a key goal for the organizational restructuring.
note: Focuses on internal productivity vs potential.

description_en: Annual assessment of an employee's performance.
example: My yearly review is scheduled for next Tuesday.

description_en: A policy of giving the most severe punishment possible for every crime of a particular type.
example: The company has a zero-tolerance policy towards harassment.

description_en: The practice of regularly staying away from work or school without good reason.
example: High rates of absenteeism can significantly reduce team productivity.

description_en: Evaluate or check (something) by comparison with a standard.
example: We use benchmarking to compare our sales performance with competitors.
note: Setting standards based on industry leaders.

meaning: nhà thầu; nhân viên hợp đồng
description_en: A person or company that undertakes a contract to provide materials or labor to perform a service or do a job.
example: The company hired a graphic design contractor for the project.

description_en: The practice or quality of including or involving people from a range of different social and ethnic backgrounds.
example: Diversity and inclusion are priorities for our human resources team.

description_en: The making of a judgment about the amount, number, or value of something; assessment.
example: The final evaluation will determine if the pilot program was successful.

description_en: A person who works as a writer, designer, performer, or the like, selling work or services by the hour, day, job, etc.
example: He works as a freelancer for several tech magazines.

description_en: A search of a person to identify and approach suitable candidates for a particular job.
example: A headhunter approached her for a senior management position.

description_en: Tax levied directly on personal income.
example: A percentage of your salary is deducted for income tax every month.

description_en: The probability that an individual will keep their job.
example: Job security is a major concern during an economic recession.

description_en: An organized association of workers formed to protect and further their rights and interests.
example: The labor union negotiated for better pay and working conditions.

description_en: A person responsible for controlling or administering all or part of a company or similar organization.
example: He was promoted to the position of project manager last month.

description_en: Discussion aimed at reaching an agreement.
example: Competitive salary negotiation is common during the hiring process.

description_en: Placement of a person in a new job, typically done by a former employer after redundancy.
example: The company offered outplacement services to employees who were laid off.

description_en: The action of promoting someone or something to a higher position or rank.
example: She received a well-deserved promotion to senior analyst.

description_en: The state of being no longer needed or useful.
example: He was offered a voluntary redundancy package when the office closed.
note: Leaving a job because the role is gone.

description_en: Personal attributes that enable someone to interact effectively and harmoniously with other people.
example: Communication and teamwork are essential soft skills.

description_en: A person who oversees a person or activity.
example: Report any technical issues to your immediate supervisor.

meaning: thực tập sinh; nhân viên tập sự
description_en: A person undergoing training for a particular job or profession.
example: The management trainee program lasts for twelve months.

description_en: Incorporating the latest developments and trends.
example: It's important to keep your technical skills up-to-date.

description_en: A fixed regular payment made by an employer to an employee, especially to a manual or unskilled worker which is typically paid daily or weekly.
example: The minimum wage has been increased recently.
note: Often refers to hourly or weekly pay.
